title: xeraea diffusa
intro: Xeraea diffusa, also known as Yerba de los Muertos, is a perennial shrub native to South America, particularly in the Andes mountains, and is a member of the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Xeraea diffusa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds, among others. In herbal preparations, Xeraea diffusa is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional remedies for its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
